_comment: Entry to add to modelcontextprotocol/servers upstream. Upstream has historically used a bulleted README list (Community Servers section) but has been restructured repeatedly in 2025-2026. Pick the variant that matches the CURRENT state of main before editing. All three formats below are semantically equivalent — Wael only commits ONE of them to the upstream repo.
variant_a_readme_bullet: - **[Blockchain Academics](https://github.com/blockchainacademics/bca-mcp)** — Canonical crypto MCP: 99 tools across news, entities, academy lessons, market data, on-chain, sentiment, proprietary indicators, and agent-backed research generation. Every response carries `cite_url`, `as_of`, and `source_hash` for ground-truth attribution. `npx -y @blockchainacademics/mcp`
variant_b_readme_table_row: | [Blockchain Academics](https://github.com/blockchainacademics/bca-mcp) | `@blockchainacademics/mcp` | Canonical crypto MCP — 99 tools spanning news, entities, academy, market data, on-chain, sentiment, proprietary indicators, and agent-backed generation. Cited, timestamped, hashed responses. |

variant_c_manifest_entry.name: Blockchain Academics
variant_c_manifest_entry.description: Canonical crypto MCP — 99 tools spanning news, entities, academy, market data, on-chain, sentiment, proprietary indicators, and agent-backed generation. Wraps api.blockchainacademics.com with attribution envelope (cite_url, as_of, source_hash) on every tool response.
highlights: 99 tools across 19 categories (news, market data, on-chain, sentiment, proprietary indicators, agent-backed generation, directories, fundamentals, chain-specific, microstructure, narrative, regulatory, security, memes, compute/AI, services, history, corpus meta, memos/theses/social/currencies)
Attribution envelope on every response: cite_url, as_of (time-travel snapshot), source_hash (content integrity)
Graceful degradation for pending integrations: stub tools return {status: 'integration_pending', reason, eta} instead of failing
Zero runtime crashes — all errors surface as MCP responses with isError: true

decision_notes: If upstream README.md on main uses a plain bulleted list under 'Community Servers', use variant_a_readme_bullet.
If upstream switched to a markdown table with columns (Server | Package | Description), use variant_b_readme_table_row.

If upstream migrated to a JSON/YAML manifest (e.g. community-servers.json) for programmatic listing, adapt variant_c_manifest_entry to the current schema. The fields in variant_c are a superset — prune to match.
In all cases: keep the diff to a single file and a minimal addition (1 line for README variants; 1 entry for manifest variants). Reviewers reject mega-diffs.
